summary: Notification Expansion Pack testing completed. Fixed 2 bugs: 1) AdminLayout was not using NotificationBell component (was using static Bell icon), 2) Admin NotificationBell was using wrong token key (admin_token instead of konekt_admin_token). Backend APIs verified working for admin/customer. Partner notifications fail due to JWT secret mismatch (PARTNER_JWT_SECRET vs JWT_SECRET) - this is a critical bug for partner notifications.

critical.issue: Partner tokens (signed with PARTNER_JWT_SECRET) fail auth on notification routes which use JWT_SECRET. notification_routes.py only supports main JWT_SECRET and looks up users in db.users, not db.partner_users

integration_issues.issue: NotificationBell component present but API calls fail with 401 due to JWT secret mismatch

notification_api_unread_count.status: PASS for admin/customer, FAIL for partner
notification_api_unread_count.notes: Returns {count: N} correctly for admin and customer tokens

notification_api_list.status: PASS for admin/customer, FAIL for partner
notification_api_list.notes: Returns list with priority, entity_type, action_key fields

notification_api_mark_all_read.notes: Marks all notifications as read for current user

customer_portal_notification_bell.notes: NotificationBell visible in header, dropdown works, shows empty state correctly

partner_portal_notification_bell.status: PARTIAL - UI present but API fails
partner_portal_notification_bell.notes: NotificationBell component present in sidebar but API calls return 401 due to JWT mismatch

admin_portal_notification_bell.notes: NotificationBell integrated into AdminLayout header. Shows count badge, dropdown with priority styling

priority_styling.notes: URGENT shows red badge (bg-red-100, text-red-700), HIGH shows amber badge (bg-amber-100, text-amber-700), NORMAL has no badge

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix notification_routes.py to support partner tokens - needs to decode with PARTNER_JWT_SECRET and lookup in db.partner_users for partner roles
Alternatively: Create separate /api/partner-notifications routes that use partner auth
critical_code_review_comments: notification_routes.py uses single JWT_SECRET - needs multi-secret support for partner tokens
Partner auth uses different JWT secret (PARTNER_JWT_SECRET) and different user collection (partner_users) - notification routes need to handle this
AdminLayout was missing NotificationBell integration - was just showing a static Bell icon with no functionality
Admin NotificationBell was reading wrong localStorage key (admin_token instead of konekt_admin_token)

success_rate.frontend: 90% (Customer and Admin NotificationBell working, Partner partial)

seed_data_creation: Created 3 test notifications (urgent, high, normal priority) to verify styling - cleaned up after testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Partner notification routes need JWT secret fix. The notification_routes.py currently only supports the main JWT_SECRET. Partners use PARTNER_JWT_SECRET and are stored in partner_users collection. After fix, partner notification tests should pass. Admin and Customer notification flows are fully working now.

issue_1.root_cause: AdminLayout.js line 242-245 was using a static Button with Bell icon instead of the NotificationBell component
issue_1.fix: Imported NotificationBell from components/admin and replaced static Button

issue_2.problem: Admin NotificationBell showing no notifications despite API returning data
issue_2.root_cause: NotificationBell.jsx was reading localStorage.getItem('admin_token') but AdminAuthContext stores token as 'konekt_admin_token'
issue_2.fix: Updated to check both 'konekt_admin_token' and 'admin_token' for backward compatibility

issue_3.root_cause: notification_routes.py uses JWT_SECRET for token validation but partners use PARTNER_JWT_SECRET. Also looks up user in db.users instead of db.partner_users
issue_3.fix: PENDING - notification_routes.py needs to support multiple JWT secrets and user collections
